locked_design.phase_1: Faithful Remaster — modern tech, original content/flow/balance
locked_design.phase_2: Stabilization Audit — evaluate every vestigial design choice on its own merits
locked_design.phase_3: Modifications — apply Phase 2 audit + new content/features + the redesign described below
locked_design.content_accuracy_method: Multi-source: emulation files + YouTube playthroughs + wikis + scripts + related-work context
locked_design.legal_posture: Fan work / clean-room, for skill development. No original code, no original assets, user owns original game as reference
locked_design.project_relationship: Standalone project. Lessons from D:\Game Design\TacticalRPG and D:\Game Design\TACTICA are reference material, not constraints. No codebase reuse.
locked_design.lineage_integration: Phase 3 collapses original A/B plotline branches into a single central loop that hits all major events
locked_design.minigames: Removed/ignored in Phase 3; design space freed for main story
locked_design.main_cast_focus: 6 main characters with substantially expanded interaction with their supporting cast (vs. original's 'silent recruitment' pattern)
locked_design.story_terminology: Use 'Chapter' for the 10-beat party-progression structure. Use 'arc' for the original Chrono Cross's story sections when referenced.
locked_design.pip_mechanic: Reframed. During Lynx form, Devil Pip grants Lynx's dark techs (Feral Cats, Hell's Fury, Forever Zero). After returning to Serge form, Herle migrates those dark techs to her own slot. Serge's light techs (Luminaire, Heaven's Call, Flying Arrow) unlock only after returning to Serge's regular body, granted via Angelic Pip. Net effect: timing of access tied to story form, but all relevant tier-8 techs eventually available.
locked_design.minigame_scope_locked: Keep only puzzles required for the narrative (e.g., Manor passcode). Optional minigames (dragon feeding, etc.) are removed. The freed design space is reallocated to expanded support-cast interaction scenes.
locked_design.language_pinned: GDScript only. No C# / .NET runtime. Confirmed.
locked_design.prng_strategy: Derived PRNGs scoped by tag (combat, dialog, treasure, etc.) so test inspection can identify which subsystem consumed entropy. Confirmed. Reasoning: 'we will have a lot of debugging to do' (user).
locked_design.augmentation_strategy: TechAugmentation as data only, no custom_handler. Tech execution triggers a scene (party completing the move, voice clip, visual effect) alongside the technical effects. Confirmed.
locked_design.chapter_transitions: Data only (ChapterData resource). No post-transition script. Confirmed.
locked_design.save_file_format: Text .tres (ResourceSaver default). Diffable, lintable, easy to migrate. Confirmed.
locked_design.cinematic_system: Data only (list of CinematicBeat resources: DIALOG, CAMERA_MOVE, ANIMATION, FLAG_SET, PARTY_CHANGE). No GDScript beat hook. Confirmed.
locked_design.source_acquisition: Fully automated. No license gate. The project is purely a fan project without commercial purpose, used for skill development. The agent may complete these actions (assuming they don't require financial payment).
locked_design.asset_extraction_tooling: Python with Pillow (TIM→PNG), ffmpeg (audio/video), timidity++ (MIDI→OGG). Confirmed.
locked_design.translation_pipeline_granularity: One script per character / per tech (extreme granularity). Confirmed.
locked_design.visual_regression_baselines: Large set: every scene, 200+ baselines, ~500 MB committed to the project. D: drive has the space. Confirmed.
locked_design.phase_2_audit_trigger: 30-day stability gate: Phase 1 must be faithfully remastered and stable for 30 consecutive days of agent playthrough before Phase 2 begins. Confirmed.
locked_design.agent_working_set_order: State first (loop_state.json), then design documents (remaster_engine_design_spec.md), then call needed skills before moving to the next decision. Confirmed.
locked_design.fix_log_strategy: Multiple files for different categories. Only the final successful fix is in loop_memory.md. Failures and individual attempts are documented in separate files (e.g., FIXES.md, FAILURES.md, ISSUES.md). Confirmed.
locked_design.tdd_commit_granularity: One commit per TDD cycle (one test passing + the fix that made it pass). Confirmed.
locked_design.user_daily_review_touchpoint: Loop summary in the user's chat. Confirmed.
locked_design.mid_loop_interrupt_handling: When the user interrupts with an out-of-band message, complete the current task and then respond to the user. Prioritizes task completion over immediate user communication. Confirmed.
locked_design.memory_file_retention: Append forever, no rotation. Revisit when the file exceeds 100 KB. Confirmed.
locked_design.ps1_emulator_default: DuckStation, configurable via REMASTER_PS1_EMULATOR env var. Confirmed.
locked_design.color_conversion_strategy: Direct expansion (5-bit → 8-bit by left-shifting) for sprites/UI; perceptual (Lab/OKLab) for backgrounds. Confirmed.
locked_design.no_game_over_flag_timing: Keep the original's trigger (after final boss's death animation). Confirmed for Phase 1 faithfulness.
locked_design.visual_approach_locked: 2D sprite for all named characters, alongside the combat sprites (in 2D battle). Backgrounds, scenes, etc. from the original game's beautiful 2D environments. No 3D module. The 2D-over-2.5D backgrounds approach (HD-2D style) is the locked visual style.
locked_design.engine_comparison_static: Once a system is decided, stay with it. No re-evaluation triggers for Unity 2023 LTS, no UE 5 alternative column. §5.3 is a static snapshot.
locked_design.form_change_chapter_boundary_locked: Form-change at chapter 4, form-return at chapter 6. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §12.4 and §12.6.
locked_design.norris_recruitment_locked: Norris appears as a nonplayable character (NPC) helping to resolve the Kid illness arc before formally joining the party. The recruitment follows logically from the body-transformation event. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §12.11 and the Kid illness arc.
locked_design.level_pacing_curve_locked: Linear curve, ~1.5 levels/chapter from 1 to 10–12 across the 10-chapter structure. Random encounters are removed/condensed into 'set piece' fights per §3.12, so the curve assumes no random encounter XP source. All elemental slot tiers should be unlocked by the end-game via the level-based progression. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §12.15 and the level scaling curves in §7.
locked_design.ng_plus_endings_locked: The 'on tracks' (canonical/preferred) ending is reached by following the central plotline. Alternative endings (e.g., the original 'let her go' ending, the new 'bring her back' ending) are accessible as optional post-game activities that summarize the alternative paths the game could have taken. This avoids requiring a replay to see all endings while preserving the single-track narrative experience. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §12.13 and the post-game content design.
locked_design.dark_tech_migration_locked: The dark-tech migration is a mechanical conceit: abilities obtained during the Lynx form segment are not unneeded/replaced but automatically transferred to Herle, who remains a playable character after form-return. This ensures continuity of player progress through the form-change segment. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §3.7 and §12.6.
locked_design.element_system_ui_locked: Element system is surfaced on a dedicated character screen. Each character has a switchable view showing: (a) their character image/portrait, (b) their elemental grid (the slots that have unlocked via level progression), (c) the ability to choose a slot, select an available tech from the unlocked pool, and place it there, (d) the 'support' techs in their available slots that can either be left alone or replaced with another tech at the player's decision. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects the §13.7 / §3.10 element-UI working assumption.
locked_design.music_handling_locked: Placeholder music only. The project is fan-only, used for skill development, and not released externally. No licensing of original music; no commissioned new music. Locked per user response 2026-07-20. Affects §2.4 and the music pipeline in §8.3.
locked_design.fan_work_posture_reinforced: All licensing decisions reaffirmed: this is a fan-only project for skill development, not commercial, not externally released. The 'no financial payment' constraint on automated source acquisition (§8.2 Decision C) extends to all other forms of commercial interaction (no licensing fees, no commissioned work, no commercial asset purchases). Locked per user response 2026-07-20.

phase_3_redesign.magic_tier_system: 8 magic tiers per character. Elemental spell slots are unlocked by character level, not by player choice. On average, each character starts with 1 slot at tier 1 at level 1; level 3 gives 2 slots at tier 1; level 5 gives 2 at tier 1, 1 at tier 2; level 6 adds 1 more at tier 1; level 7 adds 1 at tier 2; level 8 adds 1 at tier 3; level 9 adds a 4th slot. This continues up to 8th tier elemental grids. The level-based progression is part of the locked design — it replaces the earlier 'open grid slot' concept.
phase_3_redesign.open_grid_slot_purpose: REMOVED. The original 'open grid slot for player choice' concept is replaced by level-based progression. Personalization comes from which support techs are attached to which base, not from choosing which slots to fill.
phase_3_redesign.basic_attack_line: Each base has a basic attack that scales with the magic tier system. Always present, always relevant, defines base identity.
phase_3_redesign.support_tech_role: Augment the basic attack line with features (status effects, buffs, debuffs) rather than replacing it with separate actions. A tier-3 support tech might modify the basic attack to also apply a status. Tech execution should trigger a scene (party completing the move, voice clip, visual effect) alongside the technical effects.
phase_3_redesign.feature_mining: Techs that are 'just damage' become candidates for mining underused status effects from the system's design space. This is how 'boring' techs gain identity without inventing new mechanics.
phase_3_redesign.tech_design_granularity: Per character / per tech (extreme granularity). One translation script per character or per tech, per the §8.4 decision.
phase_3_redesign.estimated_tech_count: ~60 fixed techs + per-base progression of elemental slots up to 8th tier. Actual number of available techs grows with character level across the level-based progression.
phase_3_redesign.chapter_structure: 10 chapters of party progression (originally discussed as 'acts', now renamed to 'chapters'). Each chapter adds to the active party or changes its composition. Order: Serge→Kidd→Nikki→Glenn→(form)→Herle→Norris. Confirmed.
phase_3_redesign.active_party_size: 6 (up from original 3). Slot unlocks at chapter boundaries create dramatic progression reveals.
phase_3_redesign.support_combining_rules: 3 explicit combined units: Leena+Poshul, Korcha+Macha, Turnup+NeoFio. All other 33 supports are standalone.
phase_3_redesign.locked_techs_for_supports: Three techs per support, emulated as closely as possible from the original game. The full element catalog from the original Chrono Cross wiki is the authoritative reference (see `element_catalog` below).

sections.title: The Core Problem: Why AI-Agent Remasters Are Fundamentally Different

docx_generation.note: PATH may not be refreshed in current bash session. Cron jobs (fresh shells) should see it. Cron prompt includes both short and full-path commands as fallback.

behavior: 1. Read loop_state.json, loop_memory.md, review.md, remaster_engine_design_spec.md to understand current state
2. If review.md has decisions that block current work, advance to next section that doesn't need them
3. Draft or continue the next section following locked design and existing document voice
4. Append new section to remaster_engine_design_spec.md using unique section header
5. Update loop_state.json: mark section drafted, advance current_section_in_progress, set next_section_to_draft
6. Update loop_memory.md with anything learned this loop that future loops need
7. If a decision is needed, append to review.md (don't make the call)
8. Send summary message to this chat with what was produced, what's next, and any decisions needed
9. If no progress can be made (everything blocked on review), log 'idle' status in loop_state.json and stay quiet

last_loop_action: 15th consecutive idle tdd-cron loop; test queue empty, ISSUES.md empty, 22/22 tests pass; per protocol WHEN TO BE IDLE
